data.title: ENS Announces Upcoming Launch of ENSv2 for Enhanced Domain Infrastructure
data.description: Ethereum Name Service (ENS) has announced plans to introduce ENSv2, a new domain infrastructure designed for large-scale integration and subdomain expansion. According to BlockBeats, the announcement was made on April 23, highlighting the advancements aimed at improving the scalability and functionality of domain services within the Ethereum ecosystem.

data.title: ILITY Secures $2 Million Funding to Advance Web3 Cross-Chain Identity Mission
data.description:
ILITY has successfully raised $2 million at a valuation of $21 million, with Animoca Brands and Duck DAO leading the investment round. According to ChainCatcher, this funding marks a significant milestone in ILITY's mission to redefine cross-chain identity and data sovereignty within the Web3 space. The project is developing a high-performance Layer-1 blockchain based on zero-knowledge proofs (ZKP) to enable secure cross-chain data verification while safeguarding sensitive information.

The newly acquired funds will accelerate protocol development, expand ecosystem partnerships, and strengthen ILITY's position as a core infrastructure layer for a decentralized future.

data.title: U.S. April S&P Global Composite PMI Reaches Three-Month High
data.description: The U.S. April S&P Global Composite PMI Flash recorded a three-month high at 52.0. According to NS3.AI, the Services PMI Flash was reported at 51.3, while the Manufacturing Output Index Flash reached 55.7. Additionally, the Manufacturing PMI Flash was noted at 54.0.

data.title: US lawmakers have introduced an AI surveillance bill that proposes to mandate a legal writ system to restrict federal agencies' access to data.
data.description: U.S. Representatives Thomas Massie and Lauren Boebert jointly introduced the "Surveillance Accountability Act," which proposes requiring U.S. federal agencies to obtain a writ of attorney before using artificial intelligence for data analysis and surveillance. The bill aims to close loopholes in the "third-party doctrine," a legal framework originating from 1970s case law that allows the U.S. government to access user data from third-party platforms such as banks and telecommunications companies without a writ. The proponents argue that this doctrine has been overextended in the internet and AI era, weakening the protection of citizens' privacy. Furthermore, the bill addresses biometric surveillance and automated license plate recognition systems, emphasizing that large-scale data collection could create "mosaic" personal profiles, leading to deeper privacy risks. The bill also proposes granting individuals the right to sue the U.S. government for violating the Fourth Amendment. Supporters say the proposal is expected to restore judicial oversight; opponents argue that the writ requirement could affect law enforcement efficiency. Against the backdrop of continuously increasing AI-driven surveillance capabilities, the legislative battle over privacy and security in the United States is intensifying. (Decrypt)

data.title: Spain busts pirate comics website, seizes encrypted cold wallets containing €400,000
data.description: Spanish police have seized two cryptocurrency cold wallets containing approximately €400,000 (about $467,000) in a crackdown on an illegal comic book distribution platform. The operation, conducted by police in Almería, Spain, has resulted in the arrest of three suspects and the dismantling of what was known as the country's largest Spanish-language pirate comic website. The platform reportedly offered free pirated content since 2014, primarily profiting through advertising, and had accumulated over €4 million in revenue over the past decade. Notably, the cold wallets were hidden inside a thermometer located within a firewall. Police have not yet disclosed whether they have obtained the private keys or access to the funds. (Cointelegraph)

data.title: Coinbase upgrades its anti-fraud system: integrating machine learning and rule engines to reduce response time to just a few hours.
data.description: Coinbase stated that it is optimizing the rule creation process in its anti-fraud system by integrating machine learning models and a rule engine to achieve more efficient risk management. It also proposed a dual-track strategy: "models handle long-term defense, rules handle rapid response," and built a unified framework to create a feedback loop: rules are used to capture new types of fraudulent behavior and inversely train the model, thereby continuously improving overall defense capabilities. Specifically, Coinbase has significantly improved efficiency by restructuring data structures, automating schema evolution, and introducing notebook-based analytics tools, transforming the previously manual rule creation process into a data-driven and automated recommendation process. Rule backtesting performance has improved by more than 10 times, and overall response time has been reduced from days to hours. Furthermore, the new system uses machine learning to recommend parameters, helping to reduce false positives and minimizing the impact on legitimate users while combating fraud. Coinbase stated that its next step will be to advance event-driven automated rule generation and explore the "one-click conversion" of efficient rules into model features, further moving towards an automated risk management system.

data.title: Spanish Police Seize Crypto Wallets in Anti-Piracy Operation
data.description: Spanish authorities have seized two cryptocurrency cold wallets containing approximately €400,000 during a crackdown on illegal comic distribution platforms. According to Odaily, the operation was carried out by police in Almería, Spain, resulting in the arrest of three suspects and the dismantling of what is considered the country's largest Spanish-language pirated comic website. The platform, operational since 2014, offered free pirated content and generated revenue primarily through advertising, accumulating over €4 million in the past decade. Notably, the cold wallets involved were concealed within a wall thermometer. Police have not disclosed whether they have obtained the private keys or access to the funds.

data.title: Justin Sun Likens His Acquisition Strategy to Buffett's: Only Buy What You Use
data.description: Tron founder Justin Sun outlined two major acquisitions at the Inside the Blockchain 100 event at Binance Square. On BitTorrent, Sun said he acquired the U.S.-based file-sharing platform because it was the only real-world application where decentralized technology demonstrably outperformed centralized alternatives, and subsequently launched the BTT token to create economic incentives for uploaders and downloaders across the network's one billion users. On HTX (formerly Huobi), Sun said he moved after fund Baiyu Capital flagged the opportunity, driven largely by seven to eight years of personal use of the exchange. He acknowledged that the HTX acquisition was followed by unexpected setbacks, including the FTX collapse's broader impact on centralized exchanges and a subsequent hacking incident, underscoring what he called security's "permanent" importance in blockchain. Sun said both deals reflected a personal investment philosophy mirroring Warren Buffett's: only acquire or invest in products you use yourself.

data.title: RealGo Secures Over $3.5 Million for Meme 3.0 Ecosystem Development
data.description: RealGo, an ecosystem connecting games and on-chain utilities, has announced the completion of a funding round exceeding $3.5 million. According to Foresight News, the early-stage and strategic investment rounds received backing from Animoca Brands, Cogitent Ventures, X21 Digital, Notch VC, Becker Ventures, among other institutions and strategic partners. RealGo plans to use the funds to advance the infrastructure development of the Meme 3.0 ecosystem.

data.title: Analysis: Security vulnerabilities exposed in Anthropic and OpenAI have raised concerns about the security of AI models.
data.description: Following security incidents at Anthropic and OpenAI, market concerns have arisen regarding the security of AI models themselves. Anthropic is currently investigating the possibility that its Claude Mythos model may have been accessed without authorization. Almost simultaneously, OpenAI was also exposed for accidentally releasing several unreleased models in its Codex application. Analysts believe these incidents highlight that even AI model providers that emphasize cybersecurity capabilities still face significant security challenges. As AI is increasingly used to defend against cyberattacks, platform security and access control issues have become critical risk points. Industry experts point out that these vulnerabilities have intensified scrutiny of the security governance capabilities of AI companies and reflect that, despite the rapid development of AI technology, security systems still need improvement. (The Information)

data.title: 孙宇晨回顾与USDT合作契机：曾因提取5万枚USDT遇兼容性问题
data.description: 币安广场《区块链100人》第5期 AMA 中，TRON创办人孙宇晨回顾称，自己早期在使用USDT过程中，曾在提取约 5 万枚 USDT 至个人钱包时，因当时相关技术架构与钱包兼容性问题，未能实现正常管理，由此进一步关注稳定币转账体验。他表示，此后与相关方面沟通，推动 USDT 在 TRON 网络上发行，并向多家平台推广接入，主要基于转账效率、手续费和用户体验等方面的考量。

data.title: Meteo-France Investigates Suspected Tampering in Paris Weather Data
data.description: Meteo-France has lodged a complaint regarding potential tampering with Paris weather data. According to NS3.AI, the suspicion arose after a new Polymarket account transformed a $735 wager into nearly $14,000 on April 6. Additionally, another account converted a $500 bet into over $3,000 on an April 15 Paris temperature market. Ruben Hallali from Sereno noted that the Charles de Gaulle station recorded a sudden, isolated spike during the settlement window.

data.title: Grayscale transferred 3,817 ETH and 210 BTC to Coinbase Prime, worth approximately $25.02 million.
data.description: According to Arkham monitoring data, approximately 40 minutes ago, Grayscale transferred a total of 3,817 ETH and 210.006 BTC, worth about $25.02 million, to a Coinbase Prime address.

data.title: Expert Warns of Quantum Threat to Bitcoin Security
data.description: Bitcoin analyst James Check has raised concerns about the potential impact of quantum computing on Bitcoin's security. According to NS3.AI, Check highlighted that approximately 1.7 million BTC, valued at around $145 billion at current prices, could be at risk if a sufficiently powerful quantum computer were to break Bitcoin's cryptographic signatures. He suggested that while Bitcoin's normal turnover could gradually absorb this supply over several months, the governance surrounding vulnerable Satoshi-era coins presents a more significant challenge.

data.title: Meta-1 Coin Fraud: Robert Dunlap Sentenced to 23 Years in Prison
data.description: Robert Dunlap has been sentenced to 23 years in prison following his conviction for federal mail fraud related to the Meta-1 Coin scheme. According to NS3.AI, the U.S. Justice Department revealed that Dunlap deceived nearly 1,000 investors, extracting over $20 million by falsely claiming the token was backed by $44 billion in gold and $1 billion in art. U.S. District Judge LaShonda A. Hunt has also mandated restitution for the victims.

data.title: Nasdaq 100 Index Turns Positive Amid Market Sentiment Shift
data.description: The Nasdaq 100 Index has shifted to a positive trajectory, indicating an improvement in market sentiment. According to ChainCatcher, this change reflects a warming of investor attitudes in the financial markets.

data.title: Justin Sun Transfers Significant USDS Assets to Spark
data.description: According to Foresight News, Justin Sun's address, starting with 0x939, has withdrawn 300 million USDS from Sky over the past three hours and subsequently deposited it into Spark. Currently, he has accumulated assets worth 1.3 billion dollars in Spark, with USDS farming rewards reaching 5.38 million dollars. The USDS deposits amount to 436 million dollars, accounting for 18.72% of the deposit pool, while USDC deposits total 135 million dollars, representing 28.17% of the pool.

data.title: AI TRENDS | Google Unveils TPU 8t and TPU 8i at Cloud Next 2026
data.description: Google has introduced its latest advancements in AI technology with the unveiling of TPU 8t and TPU 8i at the Cloud Next 2026 event. According to NS3.AI, the TPU 8t is designed to scale up to 121 ExaFlops per superpod, showcasing significant computational power. Meanwhile, the TPU 8i is equipped with 384 MB of on-chip SRAM and 288 GB of high-bandwidth memory, optimized for inference workloads. Sundar Pichai, Google's CEO, stated that the company is on track to invest up to $185 billion this year in AI infrastructure, highlighting Google's commitment to advancing AI capabilities.

data.title: A whale has opened long positions in ETH and BTC with 20x leverage, and the current position is worth approximately $40 million.
data.description: According to Onchain Lens monitoring, a whale opened a long position in ETH and BTC with 20x leverage, and currently holds 17,257 ETH and 516.42 BTC, with a position value of approximately $40 million.

data.title: U.S. April S&P Global Composite PMI Preliminary Reading at 52
data.description: The preliminary reading for the U.S. S&P Global Composite PMI in April was reported at 52, surpassing expectations of 50.5. According to Jin10, the previous value was recorded at 50.3.
